problem: Queue code uses fixed columns and verification_status logic; new columns should not change queue counts or risk classification.
mitigation: No code change needed; verify queue renders and still reports expected entries.

problem: Simple tables use explicit/default columns, so new fields will not appear unless requested.
mitigation: Keep new fields out of simple/default tables for first patch.

problem: Main table is fixed-column and unaffected. Detail table includes family fields. Search uses Object.values(row), so hidden family text can affect search hits.
mitigation: Acceptable because family terms becoming searchable is useful.

problem: Source and docs resources can drift if Quarto render is not run after schema changes.
mitigation: Run Quarto render and verify docs/data header plus docs/site-tables.js labels.

problem: Adding columns is backward-compatible for name-based readers but can break consumers assuming exactly 51 columns or fixed column positions.
mitigation: Append columns only at the end, document the schema change, and recommend name-based column selection.

problem: Semantic drift can occur if primary CSV values differ from review artifacts.
mitigation: Keep first patch limited to spec rows and retain unresolved rows in review artifacts.
